frameworks.name: population receptive field (pRF)
frameworks.one_line: neuroimaging estimate of cortical receptive-field properties from visual stimulation

frameworks.name: plasticity vs stability
frameworks.one_line: debate over whether adult visual cortex circuits can remap or remain fixed

frameworks.name: computational / mechanistic model
frameworks.one_line: proposed quantitative model linking pRF dynamics to underlying circuit mechanisms

core_question: How should we interpret pRF changes seen in clinical disorders given that pRFs also shift in healthy adults?
key_assumptions: pRFs reflect underlying cortical neural circuitry
healthy-baseline pRF dynamics constrain interpretation of patient data
a mechanistic model is required to adjudicate plasticity-vs-stability
manipulations: comparison across ophthalmologic and neurologic patient groups vs healthy adults
review of pRF measurement contexts that elicit dynamic changes
key_findings: pRF estimates change in healthy adults as well as in patients
the field disagrees on whether these changes reflect true plasticity or measurement dynamics
interpretations: clinical pRF changes can only be interpreted once healthy pRF dynamics are mechanistically defined
mechanistic models with quantitative predictions are needed to resolve the plasticity-vs-stability debate
limitations_noted: controversy over how underlying neural circuitry operates remains unresolved
